subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Card Types
- **Exploding Kitten cards** — 4 cards (one fewer than the number of possible players).
- **Defuse cards** — 6 cards total.
- **Attack cards** — 4 cards.
- **Skip cards** — 4 cards.
- **Favor cards** — 4 cards.
- **Shuffle cards** — 4 cards.
- **See the Future cards** — 5 cards.
- **Nope cards** — 5 cards.
- **Cat cards** — 5 types with 4 cards each (20 total): Tacocat, Rainbow-Ralphing Cat, Hairy Potato Cat, Beard Cat, Cattermelon.

#### Total
- **Deck size** — 56 cards in the base game.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Table Setup
- **No board** — Exploding Kittens has no game board.
- **Draw pile** — Place the shuffled deck face down in the center of the table.
- **Discard pile** — Leave space next to the draw pile for played cards.
- **Player hands** — Each player holds their hand of cards privately.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Dealing
1. **Remove Exploding Kittens** — Take all Exploding Kitten cards and all Defuse cards out of the deck.
2. **Deal Defuse cards** — Give 1 Defuse card to each player.
3. **Shuffle remaining Defuse cards** — Put the leftover Defuse cards back into the deck.
4. **Deal hand** — Deal 7 cards face down to each player (so each player starts with 8 cards including their Defuse).
5. **Insert Exploding Kittens** — Insert enough Exploding Kitten cards back into the deck so there is 1 fewer than the number of players.
6. **Shuffle the deck** — Shuffle the draw pile thoroughly.

#### Example
- **4 players** — Insert 3 Exploding Kitten cards into the deck.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Player
- **Rulebook suggestion** — The player who most recently touched a real cat goes first, or choose randomly.
- **Turn direction** — Play proceeds clockwise.

#### Ready to Play
- Each player should have a hand of 8 cards (7 dealt + 1 Defuse).
- The draw pile sits in the center with Exploding Kittens shuffled in.
- Remaining Defuse cards are also shuffled into the draw pile.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### On Your Turn
1. **Play cards (optional)** — You may play as many action cards as you want from your hand, one at a time, resolving each before playing the next. You are never forced to play a card.
2. **Draw a card (mandatory)** — End your turn by drawing the top card of the draw pile. If you draw an Exploding Kitten, you must immediately deal with it or you are eliminated.

#### Drawing an Exploding Kitten
- **If you have a Defuse** — Play your Defuse card to the discard pile. Then secretly reinsert the Exploding Kitten card anywhere in the draw pile (top, bottom, or middle). Your turn ends.
- **If you have no Defuse** — You are eliminated. Discard all your cards face up. The Exploding Kitten stays out of the draw pile.

#### Important
- You must draw to end your turn (unless a card effect like Skip or Attack cancels it).
- There is no hand limit.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Action Cards
- **Attack** — End your turn without drawing. The next player must take 2 turns in a row (play/draw, then play/draw again). Attacks stack: if the next player also plays an Attack, the following player takes 4 turns.
- **Skip** — End your turn without drawing a card. If you owe multiple turns (from Attack), this only cancels one of them.
- **Favor** — Force another player to give you 1 card from their hand. They choose which card to give you.
- **Shuffle** — Shuffle the draw pile.
- **See the Future** — Privately peek at the top 3 cards of the draw pile without changing their order.
- **Nope** — Cancel any action card played by any player (except Exploding Kitten and Defuse). Can be played at any time, even on other players' turns. A Nope on a Nope cancels the Nope (double negative).

#### Cat Card Combos
- **Matching pair (2 of a kind)** — Play any 2 identical Cat cards to steal 1 random card from another player's hand.
- **Matching trio (3 of a kind)** — Play any 3 identical Cat cards to name a card type and demand it from a target player. If they have it, they must give it to you. If not, you get nothing.
- **Cat cards have no individual effect** — They are only useful as pairs or triples.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Nope Timing
- **Instant play** — Nope can be played at any time before an action card resolves, even during another player's turn.
- **Nope chains** — Multiple Nope cards can be played in sequence. An odd number of Nopes cancels the action; an even number lets it resolve.
- **Cannot Nope** — Defuse cards and Exploding Kitten cards cannot be Noped.

#### Defuse Placement
- **Secret reinsertion** — When you Defuse an Exploding Kitten, you may place it anywhere in the draw pile without revealing where.
- **Strategic placement** — You can place it on top to trap the next player, or bury it deep for safety.

#### Elimination
- **Out of the game** — When you explode, you are eliminated and take no more turns.
- **Cards discarded** — All your remaining cards go face up to the discard pile.
- **Shrinking deck** — As players are eliminated, both the Exploding Kitten and that player's cards leave the game, making the deck thinner.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Winning
- **Last player standing** — The last player who has not exploded wins the game.
- **No points** — There is no scoring system. It is purely an elimination game.

#### Game Length
- Games typically last 10-20 minutes depending on player count.
- With fewer players, the deck is thinner and games are faster.

#### Variants
- **Multiple rounds** — Some groups play best-of-3 or track wins across multiple games.
- **Expansions** — Imploding Kittens (adds 6th player, Imploding Kitten card), Streaking Kittens (adds hidden Exploding Kitten hold), Barking Kittens (adds shared card combos).

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Game
- **Save your Defuse** — Your Defuse card is your lifeline. Never use it frivolously or trade it away unless absolutely necessary.
- **Play See the Future often** — Early information about the draw pile is valuable and low-cost.
- **Collect cat pairs** — Stealing cards from opponents can net you extra Defuse cards.
- **Use Shuffles proactively** — If you see an Exploding Kitten near the top, shuffle before drawing.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Holding too many Nopes** — Nope cards are powerful but holding 3-4 means you have fewer useful action cards.
- **Forgetting to draw** — You must always end your turn by drawing (unless Skip or Attack cancels it).
- **Wasting Attacks early** — Attacks become more lethal later when the deck is thin and Exploding Kittens are more likely.
- **Trading away Defuse** — Never willingly give up your Defuse through Favor unless you have a second one.
- **Not counting cards** — Track how many Exploding Kittens and Defuses have been seen. This tells you the actual danger level.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Critical Choices
- **When to Nope** — Save Nopes for truly dangerous moments (opponent stealing your Defuse, blocking a critical Attack near endgame).
- **Defuse placement** — After defusing, putting the Exploding Kitten on top punishes the next player but they might have a Skip or Attack to dodge it.
- **Attack vs. Skip** — Attack is generally stronger because it forces the next player to draw twice, but Skip is safer when you just need to survive one turn.
- **Target selection** — When using Favor or cat pairs to steal, target the player most likely to have a Defuse card.
- **Late game math** — Count remaining cards in the draw pile and remaining Exploding Kittens. If the odds are bad, use every tool to avoid drawing.
